summary_one_line: ELF header whose section-header-table fields yield section_count()>1 so the GetBuildId section loop iterates instead of terminating immediately

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A valid ELF header (7f 45 4c 46 magic) whose e_shoff / e_shnum / e_shentsize section-header-table fields parse to a section count greater than 1, so the loop condition i < elf.section_count() is true on the first iteration and the loop body at lines 1280-1291 executes.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The cmplog winner seeds carry the ELF magic and structured header bytes that drive section_count() to fire 286000 times in W vs 230 in L, and the blocker true side is taken (T=228000) only in W; ReadSection and Section::header() run 287000 times in W and 0 in L, showing the loop body actually iterates over real sections.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: The naive loser seeds keep header fields that make section_count() evaluate to <=1, so the loop condition i<section_count() is immediately false: the blocker shows T=0 F=120 in L (loop never enters), Section::header()/ReadSection have 0 L hits, and instead the segment path (Segment::header() 2130000 L hits, GetSegmentName 427000 L hits) is exercised.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the per-execution CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes the comparand operands of the integer CMPs that the StructReader/section-count parsing performs on the header bytes, splicing the matching section-table field values into the input so section_count() crosses 1; naive has only havoc/token mutators and no CMP introspection, so it cannot reliably synthesize the correlated header fields.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the section-iteration loop condition in GetBuildId and its globally-blocked side is the true branch.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: for (Elf64_Xword i = 1; i < elf.section_count(); i++) { <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: Taking the true side requires section_count() to exceed 1 so the loop body executes; the body (ReadSection / SHT_NOTE check) is W-only.
The winner drives section_count() and ReadSection to fire hundreds of thousands of times while the loser invokes them zero times.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: 287000         0  elf.cc:bloaty::(anonymous namespace)::ElfFile::ReadSection(unsigned int, bloaty::(anonymous namespace)::ElfFile::Section*) const  (/src/bloaty/src/elf.cc:536-555)

evidence_trail.claim: section_count() returns >1 in W (286000 hits) but is effectively dead in L (230 hits).
In the loser the loop never enters: the blocker branch is T=0 F=120 in L versus T=228000 in W.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: T=228000 F=90  T=0 F=120  for (Elf64_Xword i = 1; i < elf.section_count(); i++) {  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: Winner seeds carry the ELF magic plus structured header bytes (offsets 0x05-0x0f) that differ from loser seeds in the parsed header region.
cmplog adds I2SRandReplace, which substitutes one logged CMP operand at input offsets matching the other operand.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: naive has no I2S mutator, only havoc/token operators.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 90 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed ecb713059f708e8e` and observed 2 I2S-floor dash rows (mutation_op = -) at depths 7 and 8 of the 9-level ancestor chain; pair is cmplog vs naive so the dash signal is I2S-exclusive per the 10-fuzzer caveat.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic ELF harness where naive resolves the section-iteration loop as fast as cmplog when the section-count fields are NOT gated behind correlated integer CMPs on header bytes (e.g. section_count derived from input length alone), showing I2S substitution is not what enables the winner.

weakest_evidence_point: The exact header offsets that I2S substitutes to lift section_count() above 1 are inferred from the broad byte-diff region (0x05-0x1f) rather than pinned to a single section-table field, since StructReader parsing spans the Ehdr.
